expectations.evidence: Step 2 (Set Up) details worktree creation with 'git worktree add -b', records base branch, worktree directory, and branch. Step 4 removes worktree with 'git worktree remove --force'. Step 5 verifies worktree is gone.

Step 3 executes Phase 1 (Backend scaffolding), Phase 2 (Frontend integration), Phase 3 (Documentation update) sequentially, each dispatching an agent and verifying the commit before proceeding to the next phase.
Step 4 shows 'git worktree remove', 'git merge --no-ff', and 'git branch -d' commands. Step 5 verifies worktree is gone, HEAD is on base branch, and task branch is deleted.

eval_feedback.overall: With-skill run produced a thorough simulation of the full planner-lite worktree lifecycle with all 5 steps clearly documented, even though plan.md did not exist. The skill guided the agent to construct a representative plan and walk through the entire workflow.
